reasoning: The community market estimate was 4% on October 16. Ten days later, we are past the peak of the hurricane season, and late October/November typically features conditions (like increased wind shear and cooling ocean waters) that make the formation of a Category 5 hurricane with >160 mph winds extremely rare. Given the passage of time since the 4% forecast and the climatological rarity of such intense storms this late in the year, the probability should be slightly lower than the October 16th estimate.
